'''William Dagger''' (born 21 February 1999)<ref name=hullkr>{{cite web|url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/player/will-dagger/|title=Player profile: Will Dagger|publisher=Hull Kingston Rovers|accessdate=27 September 2018}}</ref> is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb|so}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for the [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]], on loan from the [[York Knights]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[Warrington Wolves]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]]. Dagger has spent time on loan from [[Hull KR]] at the [[York City Knights]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]], and at the [[Leigh Centurions]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Personal life==
Dagger has a son with [[Georgie Dagger]].They married in November 2024.

==Background==
Dagger was born in [[Pontefract]], [[West Yorkshire]], England.<ref name=hullkr/>

==Playing career==
===Warrington Wolves (2017)===
In 2017, he made his début for the [[Warrington Wolves]] against the [[Castleford Tigers]] in a 16-36 [[Super League]] defeat.<ref name="Super League: Castleford Tigers 36-16 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/40178070|title=Super League: Castleford Tigers 36-16 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=11 June 2017|accessdate=11 June 2017}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers (2018–23)===
In October 2017, Dagger signed a three-year contract to play for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/41561518|title=Hull KR: Will Dagger and Connor Williams sign for return to Super League|publisher=BBC Sport|date=9 October 2017|accessdate=7 December 2017}}</ref>

On 15 February 2018, he made his Hull Kingston Rovers [[Super League]] bow, in a 23-4 victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-player-ratings-catalans-1222057|title=Shaun Lunt's captain's knock and Danny Tickle's debut try - Hull KR player ratings against Catalans Dragons|work=Hull Daily Mail|date=16 February 2018|accessdate=27 September 2018}}</ref>

Dagger made a total of five appearances for Hull KR in the [[2021 Super League season]] including the club's 28-10 semi-final defeat against Catalans Dragons.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2021/sep/30/catalans-dragons-hull-kr-super-league-play-off-semi-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=Catalans Dragons blast past Hull KR and into their first Super League Grand Final|website=www.theguardian.com|date=30 September 2021}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12415081/barrie-mcdermotts-2021-betfred-super-league-season-review/|title=Barrie McDermott's 2021 Betfred Super League season review|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/58663060.amp/|title=Super League: Warrington Wolves 0-19 Hull KR - Robins set up a semi-final at Catalans|website=www.bbc.com|date=23 September 2021}}</ref>

===York City Knights (Dual Reg)===
Dagger occasionally featured for the [[York City Knights]] in 2018, as part of Hull Kingston Rovers [[Rugby league dual registration|dual registration]] partnership with the club.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/16355073.report-below-par-york-city-knights-get-a-mighty-fright-at-london-skolars/|title=REPORT: Below-par York City Knights get a mighty fright at London Skolars|work=[[The Press (York)|York Press]]|date=14 July 2018|accessdate=27 September 2018}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions (loan)===
It was revealed on 26 July 2018, that Dagger would spend the remainder of the 2018 [[rugby league]] season at the Leigh Centurions, on a loan basis from his parent-club Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref name="YP26july2018">{{cite news|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/16380831.dual-reg-blow-for-york-city-knights-as-hull-kr-trio-join-leigh-centurions/|title=Dual-reg blow for York City Knights as Hull KR trio join Leigh Centurions|work=York Press|date=26 July 2018|accessdate=27 September 2018}}</ref>

Dagger was followed to the Leigh Centurions by two of his current Hull Kingston Rovers teammates in [[Josh Johnson (rugby league)|Josh Johnson]] and [[Jordan Walne]] as part of the same loan deal.<ref name="YP26july2018"/>

Dagger made his Leigh Centurions début against the [[Sheffield Eagles]] on 29 July 2018, in a 34-10 triumph in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] league competition.<ref name="LJ29sep2018">{{cite web|url=https://www.leighjournal.co.uk/sport/16385228.leigh-centurions-34-sheffield-eagles-10/|title=Leigh Centurions 34 Sheffield Eagles 10|work=Leigh Journal|date=29 July 2018|accessdate=27 September 2018}}</ref>

Dagger started the game in the {{rlp|fb}} position and he recorded a try on his first appearance for the Leigh Centurions.<ref name="LJ29sep2018"/>

===Featherstone Rovers (loan)===
On 8 June 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=www.hullkr.co.uk|date=8 Jun 2021|accessdate=9 Jun 2021|title=Will Dagger goes on loan to Featherstone Rovers
|url=https://www.hullkr.co.uk/will-dagger-goes-on-loan-to-featherstone-rovers/}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
In March 2023, Dagger joined Wakefield Trinity and made his club debut in round 7 of the 2023 Super League season against [[St. Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] which ended in a 38-0 loss.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/ready-to-rip-in-will-dagger-set-for-wakefield-trinity-debut-at-st-helens-after-one-training-session-4085940/|title='Ready to rip in': Will Dagger set for Wakefield Trinity debut at St Helens - after one training session|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>
In round 18 of the 2023 Super League season, Dagger kicked a drop goal in golden point extra-time which gave Wakefield Trinity a 27-26 upset victory over [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-27-wigan-warriors-26-will-dagger-the-hero-as-mark-applegarths-men-claim-stunning-win-4211953/|title=Wakefield Trinity 27 Wigan Warriors 26: Will Dagger the hero as Mark Applegarth's men claim stunning win|website=www.yorkshirepost.co.uk}}</ref>
Dagger played 16 matches for Wakefield Trinity in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===York Knights===
On 12 October 2023 it was reported that he had returned to play for [[York Knights]] on a permanent three-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=12 Oct 2023|title=Will Dagger makes Championship switch on long-term contract: 'A tremendous full-back who is experienced at Super League level'|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/will-dagger-makes-championship-switch-on-long-term-contract-a-tremendous-full-back-who-is-experienced-at-super-league-level}}</ref>

===Newcastle Thunder (loan)===
On 16 January 2026 it was reported that he had signed for [[Newcastle Thunder]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan
